name: Kindergarten World Religions & Belief — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions through a close look at family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions.

units.title: Wonder and notice: family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in family traditions, celebrations, and asking respectful questions.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
